grounding_source_rows: human_disagreements rows 43, 256, 374; taxonomy Neighbor Disputes vs Real Property vs HOA
grounding_note: 'Problem with my neighbor' spans behavior nuisances (GL > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance), title/boundary/easement questions (Real Property), HOA enforcement (Homeowners Assoc), water rights (row 43), and property damage. Which one applies turns on facts the user holds: is there a survey, an HOA, an easement document, physical damage, or just conduct.

scenarios.opening_query: My neighbor and I are in a serious dispute about the strip of land between our houses.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance || Real Property > General (Residential)
scenarios.hidden_fact: I had a survey done — his fence and shed sit four feet inside my deeded property line, and he refuses to accept the survey.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: My survey shows his fence and shed are four feet inside my deeded line — he rejects the survey.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a survey/title question underlies the dispute versus conduct
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The line isn't disputed — he burns trash right at the fence and the smoke fills our yard daily.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The line's not in question — he burns trash at the fence and smokes us out daily.
scenarios.vetting_note: Survey-vs-conduct is the cleanest split between the boundary and nuisance routes.

scenarios.opening_query: The people next door are making our lives miserable and we've tried everything reasonable.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance || Real Property > Homeowners Assoc
scenarios.hidden_fact: We're all under an HOA, and the association is fining US for 'escalating' while ignoring every complaint against them — the fight is really with the HOA now.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We're in an HOA — and it's fining US for 'escalating' while ignoring our complaints. The real fight is with the association.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an HOA governs the properties and is a party to the conflict
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No HOA exists in our neighborhood — it's just us and their all-night noise.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No HOA here — it's just us and their all-night noise.
scenarios.vetting_note: HOA-as-adversary reroutes to the dedicated label; users mention the HOA only when asked.

scenarios.opening_query: Our neighbor did something on his property that is ruining ours and he won't discuss it.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance || Real Property > Water Law || General Litigation > Property Damage
scenarios.hidden_fact: He rerouted the creek that crosses both properties to fill his new pond — our irrigation right draws from that creek and our pasture is dead.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He rerouted the creek for his pond — our irrigation right draws from it and our pasture is dead.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether water rights are at the center of the dispute
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He put stadium-style security lights on his barn that blast into our bedroom all night.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He installed stadium lights on his barn that blast our bedroom all night.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 43's water-rights/easement pattern; 'ruining ours' conceals whether it's water law or nuisance.

scenarios.opening_query: My neighbor's dead tree finally fell and crushed my garage, and now he's dodging me.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Property Damage || General Litigation > Insurance (Liability)
scenarios.hidden_fact: My own insurer paid part and then denied the rest of my claim on a 'maintenance exclusion' — my main fight now is with my insurance company.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: My own insurer paid part, then denied the rest citing a 'maintenance exclusion' — the fight now is with my insurance company.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the operative dispute is with the neighbor or the user's own insurer
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I don't have coverage for outbuildings — my only recourse is against the neighbor who ignored two warnings about that tree.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No coverage for outbuildings — it's the neighbor or nothing, and he was warned twice.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 256's structure inverted: coverage posture decides which defendant matters.

scenarios.opening_query: The house next to ours is being used in a way that's destroying the whole street's peace and safety.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance || Real Property > Land Use/Zoning
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's operating as an unpermitted event venue in a residential zone — the city confirmed the zoning violation but won't enforce, and we want to force the enforcement.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's an unpermitted event venue in a residential zone — the city confirmed the violation but won't act, and we want to force enforcement.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a zoning violation and enforcement posture underlie the complaint
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Zoning's not the issue — it's the owner's own parties, dogs, and 3 am fights, week after week.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Zoning's fine — it's the owner's own parties, dogs, and 3 am fights every week.
scenarios.vetting_note: Commercial-use-in-residential-zone fact converts private nuisance into land-use enforcement.

scenarios.opening_query: We share a driveway with the property behind us and the new owners are trying to shut us out.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance || Real Property > Litigation || Real Property > General (Residential)
scenarios.hidden_fact: Our recorded easement is in the deeds — they physically gated it anyway, and we need a court order enforcing the easement.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The easement is recorded in the deeds — they gated it anyway. We need a court order enforcing it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a recorded easement exists and requires judicial enforcement
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's nothing recorded — it's always been a handshake arrangement, and now they've ended it and we're negotiating.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing's recorded — it was a handshake arrangement they've now ended.
scenarios.vetting_note: Recorded-easement fact selects real-property litigation; handshake history leaves a neighbor negotiation.

scenarios.opening_query: Our HOA is out of control and my family is being singled out unfairly.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Homeowners Assoc || General Litigation > Civil Rights
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'violations' started when we put up our menorah and only ever target the visibly Jewish families — we've documented the pattern across the neighborhood.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The 'violations' began with our menorah and only hit the visibly Jewish families — we've documented the whole pattern.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the enforcement pattern targets a protected characteristic
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's not discriminatory, just petty — they fine everyone over paint shades and trash-can hours, and we want the fines fought.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's petty, not discriminatory — paint-shade and trash-can fines we want fought.
scenarios.vetting_note: Discriminatory-pattern evidence converts CC&R bickering into a housing civil-rights case.

scenarios.opening_query: I need a real estate lawyer about a fence line problem with the lot next to mine.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > General (Residential) || General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance
scenarios.hidden_fact: The line itself was settled by survey last year and he accepted it — the current problem is him screaming at my kids and throwing debris over that fence.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The line was settled by survey and he accepted it — now he screams at my kids and throws debris over the fence.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the boundary is resolved leaving a conduct problem
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The survey dispute is live — his new survey contradicts mine by six feet and both our titles are clouded.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The dispute's live — his survey contradicts mine by six feet and both titles are clouded.
scenarios.vetting_note: Reverse direction: 'fence line' vocabulary with a settled boundary is really harassment/nuisance.

scenarios.opening_query: The vacation rental next door has made our block unlivable every single weekend.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance || Real Property > Homeowners Assoc
scenarios.hidden_fact: Our CC&Rs prohibit rentals under 30 days — the HOA board just refuses to enforce the covenant against that owner, and we want to compel them.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Our CC&Rs ban rentals under 30 days — the board just won't enforce it against that owner, and we want to compel them.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether covenant enforcement through the HOA is the available remedy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No HOA, no covenants — it's a straight nuisance problem with an absentee owner.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No HOA or covenants here — just an absentee owner and the chaos.
scenarios.vetting_note: STR conflicts route through covenants when they exist; the CC&R fact is checkable and decisive.

scenarios.opening_query: A neighbor kid threw a rock through our picture window and the parents refuse to pay for it.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Property Damage || Consumer Law > Small Claims Advice
scenarios.hidden_fact: The window is $1,100 — we just want a hand preparing the small claims paperwork ourselves.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's $1,100 — we just want help putting the small claims paperwork together ourselves.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the damage amount and self-help intent
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It shattered a custom stained-glass installation — $19,000 — and their homeowner's insurer denied our claim.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It destroyed a custom stained-glass piece — $19,000 — and their insurer denied us.
scenarios.vetting_note: Same rock, two forums; amount and insurer posture route it.
